conditions: Registry persists only the definition layer (persona, system prompt, tuning params, opt-in distilled lessons) and NEVER live working memory or run transcripts, so per-run provenance stays certifiable
Each run clones the registry definition into a fresh ephemeral instance; the instance's working state is cleaned up at run-end exactly as in v0.3 (zero residue preserved)
Cross-run accumulated context is admitted only via an explicit, versioned, signed 'lessons' artifact that a run must opt into, never auto-injected, so 'no cross-run contamination by default' holds
Registry entries are content-addressed and version-pinned per run, so every result records exactly which expert definition version it used (reproducibility + warm-start both satisfied)
completed.keyRisk: Staleness and cross-run contamination via the registry: pinned-but-outdated expert definitions silently degrade quality, and the opt-in 'lessons' channel becomes a leak vector if discipline slips, plus added lifecycle/GC complexity
completed.position: hybrid: a persistent expert registry that warm-starts run-scoped ephemeral instances, where the registry stores reusable expert definitions (persona, tuning, distilled prior-run lessons) but every run instantiates fresh, isolated working state that is still deleted at run-end to satisfy the zero-residue certification gate
wouldChangeIf: Definition-layer reuse cannot be cleanly separated from working-state reuse in implementation, collapsing the isolation boundary that keeps provenance certifiable
Version-pinning + content-addressing fails to make registry-warm-started runs reproducible against pure ephemeral runs
The lifecycle/cleanup cost of versioning and garbage-collecting stale registry entries exceeds the warm-start latency it saves

conditions: v0.3 cleanup remains a hard certification gate that proves zero residue after every run
Cold-start cost is bounded by projecting experts from a versioned, content-addressed source at session start (custom agents load only at session start)
Any accumulated context that must survive a run is written to an explicit, auditable artifact store outside the expert, not carried as hidden in-expert state
Each run's provenance is sealed to the run scope: expert identity, source version, and inputs are certifiable per run
If warm-start is ever needed, it is rebuilt deterministically from the versioned source, not from a mutated live expert
completed.keyRisk: Cold-start cost and loss of accumulated context: re-projecting experts every run adds latency and discards warm context, risking a slow, forgetful system unless deterministic projection and an external auditable context store offset it.
wouldChangeIf: A persistent registry can demonstrate certifiable zero cross-run contamination at equal or lower audit cost than ephemeral cleanup
Cold-start latency is measured to dominate total run cost AND cannot be mitigated by deterministic projection/caching of immutable source
Losing accumulated context is shown to materially degrade decision quality in a way an external, versioned artifact store cannot recover
